name: sof-logger
description: Visualizes QEMU Logs with PS bits and Cache Misses

sofLogger.qemuPath.description: The exact binary command used to launch QEMU (e.g., 'qemu-system-xtensa' or an absolute path if it is not in your system PATH).

sofLogger.targetBuildDir.description: The absolute root directory path to the compiled Zephyr target build. The extension will automatically look for 'zephyr/zephyr.ri' and 'zephyr/zephyr.elf' within this folder.

sofLogger.qemuOptions.description: Base architectural execution parameters passed to QEMU (e.g., memory dimensions '-m 512', etc). Use this for non-machine specific settings.

sofLogger.qemuTargetOptions.description: Specific Target configuration options. This usually specifies the board, machine, or CPU requirements (e.g., '-machine intel-ptl-dsp' or '-cpu ace30').

sofLogger.qemuLoggingOptions.description: Supplemental debugging and logging parameters. Do NOT specify '-D <file>' here, as the extension automatically appends the 'qemuLogFile' path defined below.

sofLogger.qemuLogFile.description: The absolute path where QEMU will export its execution trace data (automatically appended as '-D'). The extension parses this file for the Execution Graph and Memory Map.

sofLogger.mtraceLogFile.description: The path to the text output log from the Zephyr terminal. (NOTE: The parsing mechanisms for this log are currently hardcoded internally over the framework limits).

sofLogger.bufferSizeMB.description: Maximum memory limit (in MB) allocated for nodeJS child processes, explicitly preventing heap crashes when parsing huge ELF symbol table dumps via 'nm'.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
